title: [Bug]: Metadata epoch nanosecond conversion uses floating-point timestamp math
summary: datetime_to_epoch_ns converts datetimes to epoch nanoseconds through datetime.timestamp(), which uses floating-point arithmetic and can lose microsecond precision for some timestamps.
observed: Far-future or otherwise precision-sensitive datetimes can be converted with a small nanosecond error.
expected: Epoch nanosecond conversion should use integer arithmetic from UTC-aware datetime components.
reproduction: Compare datetime_to_epoch_ns for a precision-sensitive UTC datetime with an integer timedelta-based calculation.
failing_test: Add a unit test that verifies exact nanosecond conversion for a far-future microsecond-bearing datetime.
impact: The defect can make stored timing metadata slightly inaccurate, which matters for audit trails and ordering analysis.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior or sink durability semantics change.
security: Accurate timing supports audit evidence. The fix must not introduce payload logging or external dependencies.
acceptance: The regression test proves exact conversion for a precision-sensitive datetime.
Naive datetimes remain treated as UTC.
Release notes mention timestamp precision hardening.
tests: Run focused metadata tests and full validation.
documentation: Update release notes and test report.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
